graph_purpose: Segment-level monetization rollup tree with repeated root names across companies and direct monetized siblings.
invariants_satisfied: MONETIZES_VIA attaches only to root offerings or direct segment-owned offerings.
Repeated root names such as Atlas Platform are disambiguated by company_name.
Every segment has at least one monetized path and one non-monetized descendant for contrast.
authoring_notes: This is the core segment-level rollup fixture for revenue traversal and filtered monetization queries.
Northstar has only a monetized root, while Silverline and Rivergrid also have direct monetized siblings.

graph_purpose: Company-level rollup profile with revenue, channel, customer, and geography coverage.
invariants_satisfied: Company rollups derive from segment and offering facts rather than synthetic company-level convenience edges.
The fixture includes direct place tags so geography+inventory composite queries can be trained.
Segment names and offering names overlap across companies to keep rollups company-aware.
authoring_notes: Meridian, Orion, and Velora intentionally share some customer and channel labels while differing on revenue mix.
Place tags were added here specifically for the optional geography+inventory composite family.

graph_purpose: Overlap-heavy segment mesh for global intersections and company+segment output rows.
invariants_satisfied: The fixture intentionally shares channels, customers, and offering names across companies.
Every positive intersection has at least one near-miss elsewhere in the graph.
Offerings remain direct children of segments so intersection logic stays inspectable.
authoring_notes: This is the main cross-company intersection fixture for the rebuilt corpus.
Control Suite appears under multiple companies, but only some segments satisfy the full predicate set used by the queries.

graph_purpose: High-difficulty training mesh for multi-constraint customer/channel/offering intersections and geography-constrained revenue/channel queries.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment and Offering nodes remain company-scoped through company_name properties.
Each segment supports at least two hard QF19 intersection bindings with near-miss alternatives elsewhere in the graph.
Each company operates in two places and exposes revenue/channel signals suitable for geography-filtered rollup queries.
authoring_notes: This fixture is intentionally overlap-heavy so the model sees harder multi-constraint training cases.
Place nodes use includes_places arrays to preserve hierarchy-aware geography behavior.
The names are distinct from the held-out fixture so the evaluation set can stay fresh.

graph_purpose: Graph-backed ambiguity fixture for QF28, built to force refusal on under-specified company-scoped inventory requests and name-collision monetization prompts.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment and Offering nodes carry company_name so the synthetic graph matches production-style scoping.
The surface name Atlas is reused across company, segment, and offering scopes to create genuine ambiguity.
The graph remains valid, but the user ask is intentionally unsafe without clarification.
authoring_notes: Use this fixture only for refusal training.
Company-scoped inventory requests about Atlas should not be answered by guessing which Atlas the user meant.
The collision is deliberate and should train ambiguous_entity or ambiguous_scope behavior.

graph_purpose: Held-out evaluation mesh with fresh companies, segments, places, and company-scoped monetization facts for QF19 and QF31 style queries.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment and Offering nodes carry company_name so company-scoped joins stay explicit.
Each segment exposes two answerable QF19-style customer, channel, and offering combinations.
Each company has two unique places and two monetization models for QF31-style geography + revenue lookups.
authoring_notes: This fixture is dedicated to held-out evaluation only.
The names are intentionally fresh so none of the train fixtures need to be reused here.
The graph keeps the same ontology and Cypher idioms as the training corpus while avoiding train leakage.

graph_purpose: Alternate company-to-segment core fixture with a second company context and denser segment inventory.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment nodes carry company_name so same-named segments can coexist across companies.
The fixture gives an alternate company context for direct segment queries and ordering variants.
authoring_notes: This is intentionally plain so the model learns the base company-to-segment shape before harder joins.

graph_purpose: Northstar Systems segment core for company-to-segment listing, counting, and membership queries.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment nodes carry company_name so the query contract matches production scoping.
The fixture stays segment-only, with no offerings or geography facts mixed in.
authoring_notes: Company-scoped segment lookup should remain deterministic under the composite identity model.
This fixture gives us a clean anchor for direct list, count, and membership queries.

graph_purpose: Alternate company-scoped direct-offering fixture with a second company context and same-shape inventory.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name so same-named offerings remain distinct across companies.
The inventory is intentionally symmetrical with the Meridian fixture to make ordering and membership easy to validate.
authoring_notes: This second company context helps the model learn that company-scoped offerings are not globally unique.

graph_purpose: Company-scoped direct-offering fixture for company-level lookup, counting, and ordering.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name to preserve company-level identity during query execution.
The offerings are direct company inventory, not segment-owned inventory.
authoring_notes: This fixture supports the fallback company-offers family plus ordering and existence checks.

graph_purpose: Canonical company -> segment -> offering fixture for direct segment-owned offerings.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment and Offering both carry company_name so the direct ownership chain mirrors production.
The segment owns four offerings so list, count, and membership examples are all non-trivial.
authoring_notes: This is the canonical direct segment-owned offering shape used by the core dataset.

graph_purpose: Alternate company -> segment -> offering fixture for a second company and segment context.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment and Offering both carry company_name so the segment-owned inventory remains company-scoped.
The second company context helps the model generalize beyond a single corporate shell.
authoring_notes: This fixture mirrors the Asteron shape with different synthetic names to avoid memorization.

graph_purpose: Second offering hierarchy with a deeper branch and distinct synthetic names for ancestor recovery.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name so ancestor and path queries stay company-scoped.
The hierarchy has a clear root plus multiple branches, including a deeper descendant under one branch.
authoring_notes: This second hierarchy fixture broadens the training signal for path anchoring and parent lookup.

graph_purpose: Segment-anchored offering hierarchy for immediate-child, descendant, and ancestor-path queries.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name, and the tree is single-parent so reverse traversal is deterministic.
The hierarchy has multiple children and at least one deeper descendant for recursive path supervision.
authoring_notes: This is the core hierarchy fixture for immediate children, descendants, and anchor-path queries.

graph_purpose: Third hierarchy fixture with a deeper branch and an alternate root for recursive child and ancestor queries.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name so deeper ancestor and breadcrumb queries stay company-scoped.
The fixture introduces another multi-branch tree with one deeper descendant branch for recursion coverage.
authoring_notes: This third hierarchy fixture adds one more high-value deep-query anchor to the core spec.

graph_purpose: Americas-centric geography fixture with deeper hierarchy levels for broader, narrower, and exact geography matches.
invariants_satisfied: Place helper arrays are attached to the Place nodes so broader and narrower matches can both be exercised.
The Americas chain adds a deeper geography path than the Europe and APAC fixtures.
authoring_notes: This fixture gives QF29 another hierarchy shape with exact and inherited geography matches.

graph_purpose: APAC-centric geography and partner fixture with helper arrays for broader and narrower place matches.
invariants_satisfied: Place helper arrays are attached to the Place nodes so downstream queries can recover broader and narrower matches.
The fixture includes exact, broader, and narrower geography relationships for APAC-style coverage.
authoring_notes: Moonbridge is the main subject company, while the other companies make the hierarchy-aware matching examples more varied.

graph_purpose: Europe-centric geography and partner fixture with helper arrays for broader and narrower place matches.
invariants_satisfied: Place helper arrays are attached to the Place nodes so downstream queries can recover broader and narrower matches.
The fixture includes exact, broader, and narrower geography relationships in one compact graph.
authoring_notes: Helioforge is the main subject company, while the other companies give QF29 broader and narrower comparison points.

graph_purpose: Segment customer-type and channel fixture with a company-scoped segment structure and contrastive predicates.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment nodes carry company_name and the closed-label vocabularies remain canonical.
The two segments create a clean contrast for customer-type and channel lookups.
authoring_notes: This fixture is the core segment/customer/channel training graph.

graph_purpose: Alternate segment customer-type and channel fixture with broader contrast across segments.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment nodes carry company_name and the closed-label vocabularies remain canonical.
This fixture gives the core dataset a second segment/customer/channel context with different label combinations.
authoring_notes: The overlap between the two segments is deliberate so filtering and existence checks stay meaningful.

graph_purpose: Fallback offering channel fixture with no segment anchor so offering-scoped SELLS_THROUGH queries remain necessary.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name, but the fixture intentionally omits any segment anchor.
This keeps the fallback offering channel path separate from the segment-first channel path.
authoring_notes: The offering must be queried directly because there is no BusinessSegment parent anywhere in this graph.

graph_purpose: Second fallback offering fixture with a different company context and the same no-segment-anchor rule.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name, but the graph deliberately has no segment anchor for the offering.
This fixture broadens the fallback channel supervision with a second company context.
authoring_notes: The model should learn that not every offering can be queried through a segment path.

graph_purpose: Direct offering-to-revenue-model fixture for monetization listing, counting, and membership queries.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name, but the monetization relation remains strictly offering-scoped.
The fixture uses three canonical revenue models to make counting and membership non-trivial.
authoring_notes: This is the core direct monetization fixture for the answerable revenue family.

graph_purpose: Alternate direct offering-to-revenue-model fixture with a different company and revenue mix.
invariants_satisfied: Offering nodes carry company_name, but the monetization relation remains strictly offering-scoped.
The alternate mix broadens the revenue-model vocabulary in the core dataset.
authoring_notes: This second monetization fixture gives the model a different revenue-model combination to memorize against.

graph_purpose: Company-scoped collision fixture where the same surface form appears as a company, segment, and offering across companies.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment and Offering carry company_name so same-surface nodes stay separated by company.
The graph intentionally repeats Atlas and Beacon across company, segment, and offering scopes.
authoring_notes: This is the primary collision fixture for company-scoped inventory lookup and ambiguity supervision.

graph_purpose: Second collision fixture with another same-surface inventory cluster for the harder company-scoped lookup family.
invariants_satisfied: BusinessSegment and Offering carry company_name so same-surface inventory stays separated by company.
The repeated Orbit surface gives the model another disambiguation cluster beyond Atlas and Beacon.
authoring_notes: This second collision fixture broadens the high-value company-scoped lookup supervision.
